subject: Re: Flight Engineer candidate
body:
great. we'll take the boein to lsj on the thursady the 15 from new york I think we will leave for BEdford from 
PAris , early the 12, new york that night, then st thomas on thursday 15  returning tuesday.20 gulfstream on the 24 to lsj returning april 3


----- Original Message ----
From: "LVJET@aol.com" <LVJET@aol.com>
To: JEEPROJECT@YAHOO.COM
Cc: gmax1@mindspring.com
Sent: Sunday, March 4, 2007 2:54:10 PM
Subject: Flight Engineer candidate


Jeffrey,
I would like to interview this Engineer on our next Boeing trip once we return from Europe. I spoke to Clint and like his personality and resume so far.
He lives in Orlando and is available.
He graduated the same school in Miami (George T. Baker Aviation School) during the same time as I did to receive his A & P licence.
 
Larry V.
 






AOL now offers free email to everyone. Find out more about what's free from AOL at AOL.com. 
CLINTON R. WUNDERLY
607 Woodridge Dr., Fern Park , FL 32730
321-277-3407   »   skyplummer@aol.com
 
OBJECTIVE:
To secure a position as a Flight Engineer/Technician that will provide the opportunity to make a strong, positive contribution through the application of skills and expertise.
 
CERTIFICATIONS / LICENSURES:
Flight Engineer-Turbojet Powered (6000 TT)
Ground School/Fixed-Base Simulator Instructor
Air Carrier Security/Safety Instructor
Airframe & Powerplant License
Aircraft Electrical/Avionics Diploma
FAA Class II Medical Certificate
FAR Part 125 Cabin Flight Attendant
 
TECHNICAL COURSES:
Carnival Airlines/Pilot Development Program (Dania, Florida. 1995)
Flight Engineer Course-Turbojet Po wered
U.S. Military/Aviation Logistics School ( Fort Eustis , Virginia . Dec.83-May.84)
Aircraft Electrical / Avionics Course
George T. Baker Aviation Technical School ( Miami , Florida . Sep.81-Jun.83)
Airframe & Powerplant Course
 
PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT:
B737-8OONG Ground School/Fixed-Base Simulator
B727-200 Ground School/Simulator
L1011 Ground School/Simulator
P&W JT8D-217 Engine Familiarity
 
SPECIALIZED TRAINING AND SKILLS:
•Proficient in the working relationship with MSG3 Inspection Program and Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program (CAMP).
•Trained & certified for special operations, two engine ferry on B727-200 aircraft.
•Trained & certified by Flight Safety/Boeing ( Seattle , Washington ), to instruct B737-800NG Ground School/Fixed-Base Simulator Course to qualified ATA flight crewmembers.
•Trained & certified by American Trans Air to instruct post 9/11, Common Strategy II airline security tactics along with ADVANCED TASER training to qualified flight crewmembers.
•Trained & certified by the Transportation Security Administration to instruct post 9/11,
Air Carrier security tactics to federal agency and airport personnel.
•Start, Run-up, Taxi certified and designated RII Inspector, Limited Inspections Authority and Airworthiness Release on B727, B737, and MD8O series aircraft.
 
MILITARY SERVICE:
U.S. ARMY, 1983-1987
Aircraft Electrical / Avionics Technician
•Troubleshoot & repair all electrical/avionics systems and components on military aircraft.
 
 
 
 
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE:
 
ENTERPRISE AVIATION, Orlando, Florida. Nov.05-Present 
Chief Flight Engineer / Mechanic (Corporate VIP B727-100 VALSAN)
• (Independent Contractor) Perform all operational and maintenance requirements as Chief Flight Engineer along with tracking aircraft logbook entries and maintenance records.
• Involved with preparing and representing as Chief Flight Engineer for Enterprise Aviation to achieve FAR Part 125 certificate completion with the FAA FSDO, ( Grand Rapids , Michigan ).
CAPITAL CARGO INTL. AIRLINES, Orlando, Florida. May 03-Nov.05 
Instructor / Flight Engineer Check Airman (B727-200)
•Instruct initial and recurrent simulator training consisting of normal, non-normal, and emergency procedures to company crewmembers in a full flight simulator.
•Teach entire ground school course consisting of aircraft systems, performance, CRM, TCAS, TAWS, & RVSM.    
•Administer random line checks to assure the proficiency of line Flight Engineers.
AMERICAN TRANS AIR, Indianapolis , Indiana . Jan.97-Oct.02
Fixed Base Simulator / Ground School Instructor (737-800NG) Jun.00-Oct.02
•Instruct a fourteen day lesson plan consisting of company flows, call-outs, recall items, and normal, non-normal, and emergency procedures to ATA crewmembers in a full flight simulator.
•Teach and assure the understanding and use of the aircraft's Flight Management Computer.
•Teach entire ground school course consisting of aircraft systems, performance, CRM, TCAS, TAWS, & RVSM along with post 9/11, Common Strategy II airline security tactics.
Professional Flight Engineer (LlOll, B727-200) Jan.97-May 00
•Perform all operational and maintenance requirements as a Professional Flight Engineer.
MIAMI AIR INTERNATIONAL INC., Miami , Florida . Jun.95-Jan.97
Professional Flight Engineer / Maintenance Controller (B727-200)
•Perform all operational and maintenance requirements as a Professional Flight Engineer.
•Assure the proper tracking and delegating of maintenance being performed on company aircraft while offering technical assistance to outstation support personnel.
PRIVATE JET EXPEDITIONS INC., Atlanta, Georgia. May 93-Dec.94
Quality Control Inspector / Systems Instructor (B727-200, MD80)
•Develop, maintain and teach aircraft logbook entries, required item inspection (RII), and maintenance training courses to outstation support personnel.
•Supervise and document all maintenance being performed on C & D checks at repair facilities.
•Perform vendor and contractor training audits.
AIRLINE OF THE AMERICAS (AKA) ULTRAIR, Smyrna , Tennessee . Jun.92-May 93
On-Board Maintenance Representative / Maintenance Controller (B727-200 VALSAN)
•Primarily responsible for maintenance being performed on charter operation in the field and supervise contract maintenance personnel at outstations and repair facilities.
CROSS CONTINENT (Part 145 Repair Station), Smyrna , Tennessee . Feb.92-Jun.92
Quality Control Inspector, (Independent Contractor) (B727, B737, DC8)
•Perform C & D check, primary and recheck inspections on commercial aircraft.
•Corrosion Protection and Control Program, including non-destructive testing of aircraft engines and structures using x-ray, isotope, eddy current, and ultrasonic inspection.
AIRCRAFT SERVICES INTERNATIONAL, Orlando , Florida . Sep.90-Feb.92
Lead Line Technician (Various Commercial Aircraft)
•Perform all aspects of line maintenance for several freight, charter, and commercial air carriers.
BRANIFF AIRLINES, Orlando , Florida . Nov.88-Jul.90
Avionics Technician (B727, BAC-111)
•Troubleshoot and repair all electrical/avionics systems and components on company aircraft.
